Patch Set 2:

Is this true for RHEL7.5 ?

Patch Set 2:

I'm not sure, but we're still supporting older kernels, and I really don't think getting "bool" rather than "uint" justifies the complexity of compat macros & multiple implementations.  I understand the idea in general but this seems too trivial to justify the work.

Patch Set 2:

BTW some earlier version of RHEL7.X also don't build due to rhashtable being added in RHEL7.3.

Patch Set 2:

Interesting...  I know we don't necessarily support the older kernels but it's really nice to avoid breaking them when we can.  It's very convenient for all involved to have as broad a support (at least in the sense of "still builds, doesn't immediately catch fire") as possible.

Patch Set 3:

I put a sort of lengthy comment in the Jira ticket:
https://jira.whamcloud.com/browse/LU-11454?focusedCommentId=234362&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels:comment-tabpanel#comment-234362

Basically, I'd like to consider changing the default behavior to *not* strictly bind worker threads to their associated CPT (the current patch leaves existing binding behavior in place and just adds a switch).  Maybe we can discuss in the Jira.

Patch Set 3:

Corrected link:
https://jira.whamcloud.com/browse/LU-11454?focusedCommentId=234362&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels:comment-tabpanel#comment-234362

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

(7 comments)

This should also get an update to the manual.

Patch Set 3:

Andreas,

At least earlier versions of CentOS (7.3, presumably 6.x) do not support "bool" in the MODULE_PARM statement.  So I can use the "bool" type, which would remove the necessity for the error message, but I can't use "true/false" in the input from userspace.  I'll do that unless you'd like me to write a compat wrapper or something.

Patch Set 3:

It can be changed to bool upstream where we know bool is supported.  I think it is best to code for the least common denominator: int.

Patch Set 3:

Actually, yeah, I forgot why I didn't do that before:
If we do not have a bool *parser* but use the bool type (with "uint" in the parser), we will accept input like, say "7", then convert it to bool 1/true, rather than rejecting it.

So unless we want compat wrappers, I would leave that part as is.

Patch Set 5:

I wasn't suggesting that accepting true/false input was a requirement, just an added perk of using a "bool" type.  I'm fine with that not working for older kernels, my suggestion was mainly to make it clear this is holding only a binary value, and possibly to save a bit of space in the data structures.

Patch Set 5:

Understood - The issue is that we're going to parse it as uint, so if someone gives, say "7", then we won't reject that.  It'll just be coerced to "1".  (As I understand it.)

If you still prefer bool for the parameters with that limitation, then I'm good with that.

commit.subject: LU-11454 ptlrpc: Make CPU binding switchable
commit.message:
LU-11454 ptlrpc: Make CPU binding switchable

LU-6325 added CPT binding to the ptlrpc worker threads on
the servers.  This is often desirable, especially where
NUMA latencies are high, but it is not always beneficial.

If NUMA latencies are low, there is little benefit, and
sometimes it can be quite costly:

In particular, if NID-CPT hashing with routers leads to an
unbalanced workload by CPT, it is easy to end up in a
situation where the CPUs in one CPT are maxed out but
others are idle.

To this end, we add module parameters to allow disabling
the strict binding behavior, allowing threads to use all
CPUs.

This is complicated a bit because we still want separate
service partitions - The existing "no affinity" behavior
places all service threads in a single service partition,
which gives only one queue for service wakeups.

So we separate binding behavior from CPT association,
allowing us to keep multiple service partitions where
desired.

Module parameters are added to ldlm, mdt, and ost, of the
form "servicename_cpu_bind", such as "mds_rdpg_cpu_bind".

Setting them to "0" will disable the strict CPU binding
behavior for the threads in that service.

Parameters were not added for certain minor services which
do not have any CPT affinity/binding behavior today.  (This
appears to be because they are not expected to be
performance sensitive.)

cray-bug-id: LUS-6518
Signed-off-by: Patrick Farrell <paf@cray.com>
